art_market.summary: Seoul's art scene in March 2026 is vibrant with major institutional showcases, including landmark queer art exhibitions and retrospectives. The city is increasingly recognized as a key global art hub.
new_exhibitions: Spectrosynthesis Seoul (Art Sonje Center, Mar 20 - Jun 28) - Landmark queer art exhibition
Damien Hirst Retrospective (MMCA Seoul, Opened Mar 2026)
Kim Yun-shin Retrospective (Hoam Museum of Art, Mar 17 - Jun 28)
Seo-Seoul Museum of Art Inaugural Exhibitions (Opened Mar 2026)
ongoing: Seosomun Main Branch exhibitions (PROXIMITIES, Han Unsung Collection - through late Mar)
Buk-Seoul Museum of Art showcases (Unfinished Flora, Electric Shock - through late Mar)

stock_market.summary: As of late March 2026, the KOSPI is experiencing a 'seesaw battle' driven by semiconductor/AI sector strength versus geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. While volatile, long-term outlooks from major institutions remain optimistic.

key_indicators.KOSPI_Status: Resilient following a mid-month rebound; psychological 5,600 level tested.
key_indicators.Market_Sentiment: Cautiously optimistic; technology sector dominance helps absorb geopolitical shocks.
key_indicators.Key_Drivers: AI/Memory chip super-cycle (Samsung, SK Hynix), geopolitical risk/oil price fluctuations, foreign investor interest.
key_indicators.Outlook: Analysts remain positive with year-end targets ranging from 6,500 to 7,500.
